header.title: Juan Rodriguez Donado
header.subtitle: Hello 👋🏽 I'm
header.description: Senior Software Engineer with 6+ years of experience, M.Sc in Computer Science and Teacher. Publishing tech content on Dev.to, active contributor to the open-source community.

items.title: HTMX with Bun: A Real World App
items.description: Showcases the development of a real-world application using HTMX, Tailwind CSS, and Bun, focusing on creating a service that converts Spotify links into playable previews for users without Spotify, demonstrating the integration of various web technologies for enhancing user experience.

items.title: Tower of Hanoi in P5.js + WASM
items.description: Explores the implementation of the Tower of Hanoi problem using P5.js for animation and Rust compiled to WebAssembly (WASM) to improve the efficiency of the recursive solution, demonstrating significant performance gains over a Vanilla JavaScript implementation.

items.title: Self-Hosted Password Manager with Dokku
items.description: Details the process of setting up a self-hosted password manager using Dokku and Vaultwarden, an unofficial Bitwarden-compatible server, to enhance privacy and security while managing passwords efficiently.

items.title: Remix Dashboard
items.description: Learning Management System (LMS) developed using remix, vite, drizzle, tailwindcss, and daisyui, featuring functionality for user authentication, CRUD operations for users and assignments differentiated by roles (admin, teacher, student), responsive design with dark mode, and forms validations, aimed at enhancing educational management and interaction.

items.title: I Don't Have Spotify
items.description: Find Spotify content on YouTube, Deezer, Apple Music, Tidal, SoundCloud and more.

items.title: CS Games
items.description: Computer Science Games powered by P5.js, Rust + wasm-pack and SolidJS.

items.title: URL Shortener
items.description: Web application built with Tailwind CSS, Stimulus, and Rails 5, designed to shorten URLs, featuring user authentication, link redirection, click counting, and deployment CI.

items.title: WRF BAQ 0.5km Forecast
items.description: Fetches data from NOAA (GFS) and OGIMET (METAR). Surface level observations are interpolated into the GRIB files.

items.title: Monocuco
items.description: Dictionary of slang words and phrases from the Caribbean Region of Colombia.

items.title: Mi Horario UN
items.description: Web app for students of the Universidad del Norte, manage and import your schedule into Google Calendar.

items.title: Donkey Kong: Jumpman
items.description: Game developed inspired by classic arcade mechanics, where players navigate challenges and interact in a dynamic environment, enhanced by real-time multiplayer capabilities.

items.title: Handwritten Digits Recognition
items.description: Application designed to recognize numbers drawn on an HTML5 canvas using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), showcasing deep learning techniques to accurately identify handwritten digits.

items.title: ACM Uninorte Talk
items.description: Software Engineering Abroad. Lessons learned from Canada and Germany.

items.title: Master's Thesis dissertation
items.description: Towards an Operational Data Assimilation System: A Detailed Web-Based Framework for Weather Forecast.

items.title: Adjunct Professor
items.description: Backend course (Node.js + Docker + Postgres) for the Systems and Computers Engineering program.

items.title: Bachelor's Degree Thesis dissertation
items.description: Real-time sentiment analysis of tweets by Colombian region.

items.title: Python Barranquilla Meetup
items.description: Deploy to heroku with Github Actions.

items.title: Koombea Hackathon
items.description: First place out of 8 participants in Hackathon - Backend category.

items.title: Universidad del Norte
items.description: Talk on software engineering and best practices.

items.title: Lya Electronic Hackathon
items.description: First place out of 7 participants in Hackathon - Android category.

items.title: Inca School Technology and Innovation Fair
items.description: Speaker of 'Systems Engineering'.

items.title: Uninorte IEEE Week
items.description: Conducted a workshop 'Tic-tac-toe AI using DP-Method'.

items.title: Ideaware Hackathon
items.description: Second place out of 12 participants in Hackathon - Backend category.

items.title: NativApps Hackathon
items.description: First place out of 8 teams in Hackathon - Android category.

items.title: Software Engineering in a Nutshell
items.description: Present an overview of software engineering, exploring its definition, processes, and industry insights, including problem-solving strategies, principles like DRY, KISS, and SOLID, and a glimpse into the tech industry's major companies and job landscape, designed for a seminar at Universidad del Norte.

items.title: Software Engineering abroad
items.description: Explores the opportunities and challenges of pursuing a software engineering career internationally, including insights on cultural adaptation, job search strategies, and the impact of relocation on professional growth.

items.title: WRF BAQ 0.5km Forecast
items.description: Delves into the specifics of using the Weather Research and Forecasting (WRF) model for high-resolution (0.5km) weather prediction in Barranquilla, demonstrating the application of advanced meteorological modeling for local weather forecasting.

items.title: ADR
items.description: Focuses on the importance of documenting architectural decisions in software development projects, highlighting the benefits of ADRs in improving project understanding, decision-making transparency, and long-term maintenance.

items.title: Bellman-Ford
items.description: Elaborates on the Bellman-Ford algorithm's ability to find shortest paths in weighted graphs, uniquely handling negative weights unlike Dijkstra's algorithm.

Covers an introduction to backend development for web applications, focusing on practical tools and skills necessary for modern development. It includes setting up development environments, basics of Unix shell, navigation, file manipulation, permissions, managing Docker containers, Git basics, GitHub flow, and the integration of Azure and Visual Studio Code for cloud-based development environments.
items.title: DLLO. APLICACIONES WEB BACKEND
Energy balanced optimum path determination based on graph theory for WSNs
items.description: Highlights energy-efficient routing strategies in wireless sensor networks using graph theory, aiming to enhance network longevity and performance.

items.title: Generic Types, Traits, and Lifetimes
items.description: Introduces Rust's features for code abstraction and safety, including generic data types for function and struct definitions, traits akin to interfaces for shared behavior, and lifetimes to ensure reference validity.

items.title: How to develop inside a container using VSCode
items.description: Explains the advantages of using Visual Studio Code to develop software directly inside Docker containers, leveraging the Remote - Containers extension for an isolated and consistent development environment.

items.title: Docker
items.description: offers an in-depth look at Docker, highlighting its role in providing platform-as-a-service solutions through OS-level virtualization to package software in containers, making development workflows more efficient and scalable

items.title: Deploy to heroku with Github actions
items.description: Explains how to automate deployments to Heroku using GitHub Actions, showcasing the integration for efficient and streamlined deployment workflows.

items.title: XP, Kanban, TDD
items.description: Offers an overview of agile methodologies including Extreme Programming (XP) for rapid development cycles, Kanban for visual workflow management, and Test-Driven Development (TDD) emphasizing testing before code implementation.

items.title: Web scraping
items.description: Introduces web scraping as the process of extracting content and data from websites using bots, covering tools, libraries, and methods to effectively gather data, as well as strategies to protect against unwanted scraping.
